tldr: Cerbos is a policy-based authorization engine while Clerk is a drop-in authentication platform; switching means replacing authorization logic with whatever Clerk natively offers (and likely keeping a separate authz layer).
why_switch: You want a managed, drop-in auth UI instead of self-hosting an authorization service
Your team prefers prebuilt React components for sign-in, MFA, and org management
You no longer need fine-grained ABAC/RBAC policy evaluation
You are consolidating identity and authorization into a single SaaS
You need Clerk's organization features for B2B
what_to_export: Integration code calling the Cerbos PDP
export_method: Cerbos policies live in version control as YAML — copy the policies directory and any test fixtures from your repository. Export decision logs from your Cerbos deployment per the Cerbos official docs.
import_method: Clerk does not natively replace a policy engine. Map only what Clerk supports (users, sessions, organizations, roles) using its dashboard or Backend API; consult Clerk official docs for the current import endpoints. For anything beyond simple roles, keep Cerbos or another authz layer alongside Clerk.

concept_mapping.note: No direct equivalent; modeled in your app DB
Clerk roles are coarser; review docs
Clerk emits auth events, not authz decisions

gotchas: Clerk is auth (who you are), Cerbos is authz (what you can do) — losing Cerbos may leave a gap
ABAC rules with conditions cannot be expressed in Clerk's role model
Plan how to enforce permissions in your application code post-migration
Clerk's free tier limits and MAU pricing should be modeled before cutover
Keep Cerbos running in shadow mode while you validate Clerk's role checks

email.subject: Cancellation: Cerbos Cloud / commercial plan
email.body:
Hi Cerbos team,

We are migrating our auth and access control stack to Clerk and need to wind down our Cerbos commercial usage. Could you confirm the cancellation steps, final billing, and any data export we should run before closure? We will continue to use the open-source Cerbos PDP locally if needed.

Thanks for the work on Cerbos.

Best regards
